theory.canonical_statement: ID 483 establishes the optimal control theory for the reactor driving fields. To maximize energy extraction while minimizing entropy, the system solves for the optimal trajectory of the control vector, which includes the DAANSsphere axis and phase. This ensures the feedback loop converges to the most efficient steady state. Optimal Drive: Adjusts fractal coupling strength to match the Hamiltonian tunneling component. Boundary Constraint: Ensures the system stays within the safety margin for phase locking.
